description: Evals for the skill-creator skill — covers skill drafting, test case generation, and the eval/iterate loop.

evals.name: create-skill-from-scratch
evals.prompt: I want to create a skill that helps format commit messages in Conventional Commits style. Can you help me build it?
evals.expected_output: A complete SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description), a clear Purpose section, When To Use section with trigger phrases, Workflow steps, example output format, and at least 2 test cases offered for eval.

evals.name: improve-existing-skill
evals.prompt: I have this skill but it doesn't trigger reliably. Can you improve the description to make it more likely to trigger? Here's the current SKILL.md: name: commit-formatter, description: Formats commit messages.
evals.expected_output: An improved description that is more detailed, includes specific trigger keywords and phrases (e.g., 'commit message', 'git commit', 'conventional commits'), and is more 'pushy' about when to use it — as per skill-creator guidance.

evals.name: generate-eval-cases
evals.prompt: I've written a skill for generating SQL migration files from schema descriptions. Can you write 3 eval cases for it?
evals.expected_output: 3 eval case objects in the correct JSON format with id, name, prompt (realistic user prompt), expected_output (specific and verifiable), and files array. Covers at least one happy path, one edge case, and one with unexpected input.

evals.name: skill-structure-review
evals.prompt: Review this skill and tell me what's missing or needs improvement.
evals.expected_output: A structured review identifying: missing frontmatter fields, thin or vague description, missing sections (When To Use, Workflow, Quality Checklist, References), and recommendations for improvement.
